name: Missouri Style Ribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the salt, sugar, cumin, pepper, chili powder, and paprika to make the BBQ rub.
2. Rub the mixture thoroughly over the pork ribs.
3. Place the ribs on baking sheets and cook in a 180°C oven for 3 hours.
4. Do not turn the ribs; slow cooking infuses the spices.
5. Remove the ribs from the oven. (Ribs may be covered and refrigerated up to 2 days before grilling.)
6. Use a very low charcoal fire with the rack set as high as possible.
7. Grill the ribs for 5 to 30 minutes per side, depending on heat and temperature of the ribs. Ribs should have a light outer crust and be heated throughout. (Longer cooking produces better ribs)
8. If you prefer juicy ribs, coat them with basting sauce just before removing from the grill.
9. Otherwise, serve dry with sauce on the side.
10. Remove the ribs from the grill, cut into serving sizes, and serve.
Basting Sauce: Mix all ingredients in a bowl. The recipe can be easily halved or doubled while maintaining the 2:1 proportion of sauce to ribs.
